items.title: Forget the generation gap, this budget is about 'the 1 per cent' and the rest

children.content: Just like it did in 2019, Labor is making an argument about wealth in Australia when it proposes negative gearing and capital gains tax changes.

articles.title: Find out if you're among the budget's winners or losers

children.content: In the first full budget since Labor's landslide election victory last year, the government is putting some money back in workers' pockets and increasing taxes on investors. So who wins and who misses out?

image.altText: An illustration of cards with budget measures which are "winners" and "losers"

articles.title: Budget's $600m for Bondi attack response branded 'quite modest'

children.content: The Executive Council of Australian Jewry has welcomed a $600 million federal budget funding commitment for the Jewish community, but says it is a "small comfort" for those who lost loved ones in the Bondi terror attack.

image.altText: Floral tributes on promenade at Bondi Beach

articles.title: Here's how the budget's tax changes will affect you

children.content: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

image.altText: A house with a white timber fence and a yellow for sale sign.

items.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?

children.content: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

image.altText: an older woman's hand hovers next to a calculator

items.title: Will property tax changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?

children.content: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.

image.altText: Close up of house under construction, frame and windows, no walls, no workers in shot.

items.title: How Australians across generations feel about the budget

children.content: The ABC has asked Australians across generations, from gen Z to baby boomers, about the latest federal budget.

image.altText: A graphic including four people behind a background of money and post-it notes.

items.title: Commonwealth Bank shares slump, nearing biggest one-day fall on record

children.content: As the nation digests the budget, Australian shares are trading lower, with Commonwealth Bank shares plummeting more than 9 per cent on tax changes. Follow our live markets blog.

image.altText: A Commonwealth Bank branch in Melbourne's CBD

items.title: $150m compensation payout for Yindjibarndi slammed as too little

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

gallery.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

thumbnail.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

items.title: Qld ministerial lovers and premier referred to CCC by opposition

children.content: The call for investigation follows weeks of scrutiny over whether ministers Amanda Camm and Tim Mander had made appropriate conflict of interest declarations relating to their relationship.

image.altText: A composite image of Tim Mander and Amanda Camm's headshots.

items.title: Ex-Demon May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt AFL retirement

children.content: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

image.altText: AFL player preparing to catch the football

items.title: 'We don’t want these people': Minorities fearful after One Nation victory

children.content: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

image.altText: A man wearing a black hat looks at the camera

items.title: Iran war becomes a 'double-edged sword' for China as Trump visits

children.content: As US President Donald Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing, some analysts say the war in Iran has significantly shifted the power dynamics in China's favour.

image.altText: Donald Trump's walks outside in strong wind, which blows his tie sideways.

items.title: New report accuses Hamas of 'deliberately' using rape on October 7

children.content: A 300-page report from an Israeli non-profit organisation has detailed allegations of horrific sexual assault against victims of Hamas's October 7, 2023, attacks on Israel, accusing the group of "systematically and deliberately" using rape and sexual torture.

gallery.altText: A person with an Israeli flag draped on their shoulders stands in the middle of signs with peoples photos outside

thumbnail.altText: A person with an Israeli flag draped on their shoulders stands in the middle of signs with peoples photos outside

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: US author to be sentenced for killing husband then writing a book on grief

children.content: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

image.altText: A woman in a black blazer and brown hair tied back looks over her shoulder.

items.title: John Travolta's luxury jet bound for aviation museum arrives by ship

children.content: After nearly a decade of delays and millions of dollars in repairs, John Travolta's old luxury jet needed a boat to get to Australia.

image.altText: John Travolta talks to media in front of the Connie aircraft at the Shellharbour Airport.

items.title: How Broadbeach became a blues mecca

children.content: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.

gallery.altText: Large crowd watches Blues on Broadbeach stage at night, lit by bright lights under cloudy sky.

thumbnail.altText: Large crowd watches Blues on Broadbeach stage at night, lit by bright lights under cloudy sky.

items.title: Eurovision pays musical homage to Austria-Australia confusion

children.content: The first semifinal of the Eurovision Song Contest includes a comedic musical interlude about the differences between Austria and Australia and sends 10 countries through to the grand final on Saturday.

image.altText: three singers in yellow and black jumpsuits clap on stage

items.title: The pianist fighting the MSO over right to bring whole self to stage

children.content: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, Australian man, 39, in a blazer and white shirt, concentrates as he plays piano in a concert hall.

items.title: Inquiry witness claims CFMEU planted asbestos to get project shut down

children.content: The inquiry heard the CFMEU was making social media posts attacking the credibility of WHSQ employees.

items.title: Police searching for man after woman's body found outside cafe

children.content: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup.

image.altText: A note on the cafe window where the woman's body was found.

items.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure

children.content: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

image.altText: Nathan Cleary (right) of the Panthers poses with the Provan-Summons Trophy alongside his father and coach Ivan Cleary

items.title: Miner to sue council for $22m in years-long dispute over access road

children.content: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.

image.altText: a truck drives past a house on a dirt road

items.title: Whyalla steelworks remains 'fiscal risk' in budget as SA waits for freight bypass funding

children.content: The federal government's ongoing financial support for the Whyalla steelworks remains a "fiscal risk" to the budget. Meanwhile, the South Australian government continues to hold out for more Commonwealth funding for a major road project.

image.altText: A view from near the blast furnace of the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: NT Police fraud squad seizes grenades, warheads in raids south of Darwin

children.content: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

items.title: Ship owner bid to limit payout over tugboats sinking fails

children.content: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

image.altText: A tug boat sinking in a river, with the cabin still above water

items.title: Ex-bikie boss who paid sniper to kill rival gets 34 years in jail

children.content: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.

image.altText: A man named David Pye has tattoos and smiles as he walks down stairs.

items.title: Police confirm body found in remote nature reserve is triple murderer

children.content: NSW Police has confirmed the body of a man found beside a vehicle in the state’s central west was that of triple murderer Julian Ingram.

image.altText: Police tape in focus with a backdrop of grass and trees

items.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist advised to test for bloodborne viruses

children.content: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

image.altText: A person receiving dental treatment.

items.title: British paratroopers drop onto remote island after suspected hantavirus case

children.content: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

gallery.altText: A paratrooper with equipment strapped to his front jumps out of an airplane.

thumbnail.altText: A paratrooper with equipment strapped to his front jumps out of an airplane.

items.title: Solar eclipse to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere' in 2028

children.content: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

image.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky

items.title: Inquest finds treatment of prisoner in lead up to death 'reprehensible'

children.content: Corey Desmond Devree died when he was left alone for extended periods of time at the prison's paint workshop.

image.altText: The front gate of a medium security prison.

items.title: Mornington Peninsula mourns as Sammy the seal believed dead

children.content: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

gallery.altText: A seal sits upright in front of a jetty on a beach, with a rainbow in the background
A seal sleeps on a rocky wall, looking towards the camera

thumbnail.altText: A seal sits upright in front of a jetty on a beach, with a rainbow in the background
A seal sleeps on a rocky wall, looking towards the camera

items.title: Australia axes quick as Women's T20 World Cup squad is named

children.content: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

image.altText: Female fast bowler running through after delivering the ball

items.title: 'We are heartbroken': NBA in shock after death of Grizzlies forward

children.content: Memphis Grizzlies forward Clarke has died, the team, his agency and the NBA have confirmed.

image.altText: NBA player in street clothes walking through the corridors of an arena before a match

items.title: Farmers and environmentalists label federal budget 'savage' and 'tragic'

children.content: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.
